', 'Have you been paying attention to shares of ArcelorMittal (MT Quick QuoteMT  -  Free Report)    ? Shares have been on the move with the stock up 10.4% over the past month. The stock hit a new 52-week high of $34.5 in the previous session. ArcelorMittal has gained 49.7% since the start of the year compared to the 19.9% move for the Zacks Basic Materials sector and the 52.3% return for the Zacks Steel - Producers industry.', "What's Driving the Outperformance?", "The stock has an impressive record of positive earnings surprises, as it hasn't missed our earnings consensus estimate in any of the last four quarters. In its last earnings report on July 29, 2021, ArcelorMittal reported EPS of $3.46 versus consensus estimate of $2.7 while it missed the consensus revenue estimate by 1.95%.", 'For the current fiscal year, ArcelorMittal is expected to post earnings of $10.66 per share on $70.89 billion in revenues. This represents a 1484.42% change in EPS on a 33.07% change in revenues. For the next fiscal year, the company is expected to earn $7.69 per share on $67.08 billion in revenues. This represents a year-over-year change of -27.86% and -5.37%, respectively.', 'Valuation Metrics', 'ArcelorMittal may be at a 52-week high right now, but what might the future hold for the stock? A key aspect of this question is taking a look at valuation metrics in order to determine if the company has run ahead of itself.', 'On this front, we can look at the Zacks Style Scores, as they provide investors with an additional way to sort through stocks (beyond looking at the Zacks Rank of a security). These styles are represented by grades running from A to F in the categories of Value, Growth, and Momentum, while there is a combined VGM Score as well. Investors should consider the style scores a valuable tool that can help you to pick the most appropriate Zacks Rank stocks based on their individual investment style.', "ArcelorMittal has a Value Score of A. The stock's Growth and Momentum Scores are B and B, respectively, giving the company a VGM Score of A.", "In terms of its value breakdown, the stock currently trades at 3.2X current fiscal year EPS estimates. On a trailing cash flow basis, the stock currently trades at 16.7X versus its peer group's average of 11X. Additionally, the stock has a PEG ratio of 2.1. This isn't enough to put the company in the top echelon of all stocks we cover from a value perspective.", 'Zacks Rank', "We also need to consider the stock's Zacks Rank, as this supersedes any trend on the style score front. Fortunately, ArcelorMittal currently has a Zacks Rank of #1 (Strong Buy) thanks to favorable earnings estimate revisions from covering analysts.", 'Since we recommend that investors select stocks carrying Zacks Rank of 1 (Strong Buy) or 2 (Buy) and Style Scores of A or B, it looks as if ArcelorMittal fits the bill. ', 'Have you been paying attention to shares of ArcelorMittal (MT Quick QuoteMT  -  Free Report)    ? Shares have been on the move with the stock up 10.4% over the past month. The stock hit a new 52-week high of $34.5 in the previous session. ArcelorMittal has gained 49.7% since the start of the year compared to the 19.9% move for the Zacks Basic Materials sector and the 52.3% return for the Zacks Steel - Producers industry.', "What's Driving the Outperformance?", "The stock has an impressive record of positive earnings surprises, as it hasn't missed our earnings consensus estimate in any of the last four quarters. In its last earnings report on July 29, 2021, ArcelorMittal reported EPS of $3.46 versus consensus estimate of $2.7 while it missed the consensus revenue estimate by 1.95%.", 'For the current fiscal year, ArcelorMittal is expected to post earnings of $10.66 per share on $70.89 billion in revenues. This represents a 1484.42% change in EPS on a 33.07% change in revenues. For the next fiscal year, the company is expected to earn $7.69 per share on $67.08 billion in revenues. This represents a year-over-year change of -27.86% and -5.37%, respectively.', 'Valuation Metrics', 'ArcelorMittal may be at a 52-week high right now, but what might the future hold for the stock? A key aspect of this question is taking a look at valuation metrics in order to determine if the company has run ahead of itself.', 'On this front, we can look at the Zacks Style Scores, as they provide investors with an additional way to sort through stocks (beyond looking at the Zacks Rank of a security). These styles are represented by grades running from A to F in the categories of Value, Growth, and Momentum, while there is a combined VGM Score as well. Investors should consider the style scores a valuable tool that can help you to pick the most appropriate Zacks Rank stocks based on their individual investment style.', "ArcelorMittal has a Value Score of A. The stock's Growth and Momentum Scores are B and B, respectively, giving the company a VGM Score of A.", "In terms of its value breakdown, the stock currently trades at 3.2X current fiscal year EPS estimates. On a trailing cash flow basis, the stock currently trades at 16.7X versus its peer group's average of 11X. Additionally, the stock has a PEG ratio of 2.1. This isn't enough to put the company in the top echelon of all stocks we cover from a value perspective.", 'Zacks Rank', "We also need to consider the stock's Zacks Rank, as this supersedes any trend on the style score front. Fortunately, ArcelorMittal currently has a Zacks Rank of #1 (Strong Buy) thanks to favorable earnings estimate revisions from covering analysts.", 'Since we recommend that investors select stocks carrying Zacks Rank of 1 (Strong Buy) or 2 (Buy) and Style Scores of A or B, it looks as if ArcelorMittal fits the bill. ', 'Have you been paying attention to shares of ArcelorMittal (MT Quick QuoteMT  -  Free Report)    ? Shares have been on the move with the stock up 10.4% over the past month. The stock hit a new 52-week high of $34.5 in the previous session. ArcelorMittal has gained 49.7% since the start of the year compared to the 19.9% move for the Zacks Basic Materials sector and the 52.3% return for the Zacks Steel - Producers industry.', "What's Driving the Outperformance?", "The stock has an impressive record of positive earnings surprises, as it hasn't missed our earnings consensus estimate in any of the last four quarters. In its last earnings report on July 29, 2021, ArcelorMittal reported EPS of $3.46 versus consensus estimate of $2.7 while it missed the consensus revenue estimate by 1.95%.", 'For the current fiscal year, ArcelorMittal is expected to post earnings of $10.66 per share on $70.89 billion in revenues. This represents a 1484.42% change in EPS on a 33.07% change in revenues. For the next fiscal year, the company is expected to earn $7.69 per share on $67.08 billion in revenues. This represents a year-over-year change of -27.86% and -5.37%, respectively.', 'Valuation Metrics', 'ArcelorMittal may be at a 52-week high right now, but what might the future hold for the stock? A key aspect of this question is taking a look at valuation metrics in order to determine if the company has run ahead of itself.', 'On this front, we can look at the Zacks Style Scores, as they provide investors with an additional way to sort through stocks (beyond looking at the Zacks Rank of a security). These styles are represented by grades running from A to F in the categories of Value, Growth, and Momentum, while there is a combined VGM Score as well. Investors should consider the style scores a valuable tool that can help you to pick the most appropriate Zacks Rank stocks based on their individual investment style.', "ArcelorMittal has a Value Score of A. The stock's Growth and Momentum Scores are B and B, respectively, giving the company a VGM Score of A.", "In terms of its value breakdown, the stock currently trades at 3.2X current fiscal year EPS estimates. On a trailing cash flow basis, the stock currently trades at 16.7X versus its peer group's average of 11X. Additionally, the stock has a PEG ratio of 2.1. This isn't enough to put the company in the top echelon of all stocks we cover from a value perspective.", 'Zacks Rank', "We also need to consider the stock's Zacks Rank, as this supersedes any trend on the style score front. Fortunately, ArcelorMittal currently has a Zacks Rank of #1 (Strong Buy) thanks to favorable earnings estimate revisions from covering analysts.", 'Since we recommend that investors select stocks carrying Zacks Rank of 1 (Strong Buy) or 2 (Buy) and Style Scores of A or B, it looks as if ArcelorMittal fits the bill. ', 'Have you been paying attention to shares of ArcelorMittal (MT Quick QuoteMT  -  Free Report)    ? Shares have been on the move with the stock up 10.4% over the past month. The stock hit a new 52-week high of $34.5 in the previous session. ArcelorMittal has gained 49.7% since the start of the year compared to the 19.9% move for the Zacks Basic Materials sector and the 52.3% return for the Zacks Steel - Producers industry.', "What's Driving the Outperformance?", "The stock has an impressive record of positive earnings surprises, as it hasn't missed our earnings consensus estimate in any of the last four quarters. In its last earnings report on July 29, 2021, ArcelorMittal reported EPS of $3.46 versus consensus estimate of $2.7 while it missed the consensus revenue estimate by 1.95%.", 'For the current fiscal year, ArcelorMittal is expected to post earnings of $10.66 per share on $70.89 billion in revenues. This represents a 1484.42% change in EPS on a 33.07% change in revenues. For the next fiscal year, the company is expected to earn $7.69 per share on $67.08 billion in revenues. This represents a year-over-year change of -27.86% and -5.37%, respectively.', 'Valuation Metrics', 'ArcelorMittal may be at a 52-week high right now, but what might the future hold for the stock? A key aspect of this question is taking a look at valuation metrics in order to determine if the company has run ahead of itself.', 'On this front, we can look at the Zacks Style Scores, as they provide investors with an additional way to sort through stocks (beyond looking at the Zacks Rank of a security). These styles are represented by grades running from A to F in the categories of Value, Growth, and Momentum, while there is a combined VGM Score as well. Investors should consider the style scores a valuable tool that can help you to pick the most appropriate Zacks Rank stocks based on their individual investment style.', "ArcelorMittal has a Value Score of A. The stock's Growth and Momentum Scores are B and B, respectively, giving the company a VGM Score of A.", "In terms of its value breakdown, the stock currently trades at 3.2X current fiscal year EPS estimates. On a trailing cash flow basis, the stock currently trades at 16.7X versus its peer group's average of 11X. Additionally, the stock has a PEG ratio of 2.1. This isn't enough to put the company in the top echelon of all stocks we cover from a value perspective.", 'Zacks Rank', "We also need to consider the stock's Zacks Rank, as this supersedes any trend on the style score front. Fortunately, ArcelorMittal currently has a Zacks Rank of #1 (Strong Buy) thanks to favorable earnings estimate revisions from covering analysts.", 'Since we recommend that investors select stocks carrying Zacks Rank of 1 (Strong Buy) or 2 (Buy) and Style Scores of A or B, it looks as if ArcelorMittal fits the bill. ', 'Have you been paying attention to shares of ArcelorMittal (MT Quick QuoteMT  -  Free Report)    ? Shares have been on the move with the stock up 10.4% over the past month. The stock hit a new 52-week high of $34.5 in the previous session. ArcelorMittal has gained 49.7% since the start of the year compared to the 19.9% move for the Zacks Basic Materials sector and the 52.3% return for the Zacks Steel - Producers industry.', "What's Driving the Outperformance?", "The stock has an impressive record of positive earnings surprises, as it hasn't missed our earnings consensus estimate in any of the last four quarters. In its last earnings report on July 29, 2021, ArcelorMittal reported EPS of $3.46 versus consensus estimate of $2.7 while it missed the consensus revenue estimate by 1.95%.", 'For the current fiscal year, ArcelorMittal is expected to post earnings of $10.66 per share on $70.89 billion in revenues. This represents a 1484.42% change in EPS on a 33.07% change in revenues. For the next fiscal year, the company is expected to earn $7.69 per share on $67.08 billion in revenues. This represents a year-over-year change of -27.86% and -5.37%, respectively.', 'Valuation Metrics', 'ArcelorMittal may be at a 52-week high right now, but what might the future hold for the stock? A key aspect of this question is taking a look at valuation metrics in order to determine if the company has run ahead of itself.', 'On this front, we can look at the Zacks Style Scores, as they provide investors with an additional way to sort through stocks (beyond looking at the Zacks Rank of a security). These styles are represented by grades running from A to F in the categories of Value, Growth, and Momentum, while there is a combined VGM Score as well. Investors should consider the style scores a valuable tool that can help you to pick the most appropriate Zacks Rank stocks based on their individual investment style.', "ArcelorMittal has a Value Score of A. The stock's Growth and Momentum Scores are B and B, respectively, giving the company a VGM Score of A.", "In terms of its value breakdown, the stock currently trades at 3.2X current fiscal year EPS estimates. On a trailing cash flow basis, the stock currently trades at 16.7X versus its peer group's average of 11X. Additionally, the stock has a PEG ratio of 2.1. This isn't enough to put the company in the top echelon of all stocks we cover from a value perspective.", 'Zacks Rank', "We also need to consider the stock's Zacks Rank, as this supersedes any trend on the style score front. Fortunately, ArcelorMittal currently has a Zacks Rank of #1 (Strong Buy) thanks to favorable earnings estimate revisions from covering analysts.", 'Since we recommend that investors select stocks carrying Zacks Rank of 1 (Strong Buy) or 2 (Buy) and Style Scores of A or B, it looks as if ArcelorMittal fits the bill. ', 'Have you been paying attention to shares of ArcelorMittal (MT Quick QuoteMT  -  Free Report)    ? Shares have been on the move with the stock up 10.4% over the past month. The stock hit a new 52-week high of $34.5 in the previous session. ArcelorMittal has gained 49.7% since the start of the year compared to the 19.9% move for the Zacks Basic Materials sector and the 52.3% return for the Zacks Steel - Producers industry.', "What's Driving the Outperformance?", "The stock has an impressive record of positive earnings surprises, as it hasn't missed our earnings consensus estimate in any of the last four quarters. In its last earnings report on July 29, 2021, ArcelorMittal reported EPS of $3.46 versus consensus estimate of $2.7 while it missed the consensus revenue estimate by 1.95%.", 'For the current fiscal year, ArcelorMittal is expected to post earnings of $10.66 per share on $70.89 billion in revenues. This represents a 1484.42% change in EPS on a 33.07% change in revenues. For the next fiscal year, the company is expected to earn $7.69 per share on $67.08 billion in revenues. This represents a year-over-year change of -27.86% and -5.37%, respectively.', 'Valuation Metrics', 'ArcelorMittal may be at a 52-week high right now, but what might the future hold for the stock? A key aspect of this question is taking a look at valuation metrics in order to determine if the company has run ahead of itself.', 'On this front, we can look at the Zacks Style Scores, as they provide investors with an additional way to sort through stocks (beyond looking at the Zacks Rank of a security). These styles are represented by grades running from A to F in the categories of Value, Growth, and Momentum, while there is a combined VGM Score as well. Investors should consider the style scores a valuable tool that can help you to pick the most appropriate Zacks Rank stocks based on their individual investment style.', "ArcelorMittal has a Value Score of A. The stock's Growth and Momentum Scores are B and B, respectively, giving the company a VGM Score of A.", "In terms of its value breakdown, the stock currently trades at 3.2X current fiscal year EPS estimates. On a trailing cash flow basis, the stock currently trades at 16.7X versus its peer group's average of 11X. Additionally, the stock has a PEG ratio of 2.1. This isn't enough to put the company in the top echelon of all stocks we cover from a value perspective.", 'Zacks Rank', "We also need to consider the stock's Zacks Rank, as this supersedes any trend on the style score front. Fortunately, ArcelorMittal currently has a Zacks Rank of #1 (Strong Buy) thanks to favorable earnings estimate revisions from covering analysts.", 'Since we recommend that investors select stocks carrying Zacks Rank of 1 (Strong Buy) or 2 (Buy) and Style Scores of A or B, it looks as if ArcelorMittal fits the bill.
